4 NOTICE.


In the Superior Court of the State
T of Washington, holding terms in


and for the County Of Spokane.


Chicago, Milwaukee & Puget
Sound Railway Company, q corpor-
alton, vs. McAtee-Knepp Invest
fment Company, a corporation; Ideal
Laundry Company, corporation;
F. l.. WIKI and Wiki, his
wife; Hermine Schoenfeld and Ben.
jamin Schoenfeld, Respondents.
+ The State of Washington, to the
above named respondents, F. L..
Wiki and -- Wilk, his wife;
Hermine Schoenfeld and Benjamin
Schoenfeld, Greeting:


You, and each of you, are hereby
notified that the above named pc
titioner, Chicago, Milwaukee &
Puget Sound Railway Company, a
corporation, has filed it's petition in
the above entitled court, against
you as parties owning or claiming
an interest in the property herein
after described, praying for a judg-
ment of condemnation and appro-
priation Of lot thirteen (13), in
block ten Q0), of Havermale's Ad-
dition to Spokane Falls (now Spo-
kane), In Spokane County, Wash
ington.
The objects of said petition are
to obtain decree of said court ad-
judging that the contemplated use
for which said land is sought to be
appropriated by said petitioner,
which use is for the construction,
maintenance and operation of a
certain line of railroad to be con-
structed by said petitioner, extend
Ing from a point at or near the
town of Maiden, in Whitman
county, Washington, to the city of
Spokane, in Spokane county, Wash
ington, and for the necessary side
tracks, depot grounds, terminal,
transfer and switching grounds,
and warehouses required for re-
ceiving, delivering, storage and
handling of freight with security
and safety to the public in the con-
struction maintenance and opera
ton of said railroad, is really a
public use; that the public interest
requires the prosecution of such
enterprise; that said land is re-
Quired and necessary for the pur-
poses of such enterprise; and to
secure the determination by jury
as provided by law, unless such
jury be waived, and in that event
by said court, of the damages which
shall result by reason of the appro-
priation and use of said land by
petitioner, as aforesaid, and which
should be paid to the owners and
Eall other persons, If any, interested
in said land, for the taking of said
land by petitioner for the uses and
purposes aforesaid; and to obtain
la judgment Of appropriation Of said
described land, thereby vesting the
Illegal title to the same In said petit.
p toner.


Il And you are further notified that
the said petitioner will, on the goth
of September, A. D. 1909, at the
hour of $50 o'clock a. m. Of said
day, or as soon thereafter as petit
tioner can be heard, present its said
petition to the Honorable J. d
Hinkle, one of the judges of the
above named court, at his court
room, in the county court house OF
Spokane county, in the City of Spo
kane, Washington; and will, at said
time and place, or at such time and
place as the said court may then
continue such hearing to, submit
proof as prescribed by law, Of the
matters and things alleged in said
petition.


CULLEN e DUDLEY,
Attorneys for said Chicago, Milwau


kee & Puget Sound Railway
